programmes.show_description: The Travel Writer: Manny is proud of the travel writing festival he's organised, with a guest appearance from charming explorer Jason Hamilton, but Fran soon falls for his charm.
Party: It's Friday night. Manny and Fran are determined to go out and do something for once but Bernard would rather stay at home, get drunk and ignore his friends.
Host Jimmy Carr is joined by team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson with guests Jason Biggs, Nicola Adams, Nick Helm and Rob Beckett for another episode of the news quiz.
Jimmy Carr is joined by team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson and guests Micky Flanagan, Stephen Mangan, Natalie Cassidy and Humphrey Ker for another edition of the quiz.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Armando Montelongo, CEO of Texas-based Armando Montelongo Companies, one of the leaders in real estate investment education in the US, goes incognito to work beside his staff.
Gina Butler is the founder and CEO of Gigi's Cupcakes, one of America's most successful bakeries, based in Nashville. She dons a disguise to go undercover among her workforce.
...Selling: Beth and Chris want to move to a better district, but their house is aesthetically challenged - which Jonathan doesn't help with when he uncovers bad news.
In West Yorkshire, Sophie and Harj plan a Mexican menu, but can Harj find his way around the kitchen? Richard and Libby stick to British classics. Butcher Sean and Jane serve a meat feast.
In Glasgow, there's an impromptu sing-song, a kitchen disaster and an MC battle, as three couples compete to host the best dinner party.
The One in a Hot Air Balloon: The sky really is the limit for David as he plans nuptials in a hot air balloon for bride-to-be Elle. How will she take it?
The One in a Car Park: In an attempt to avoid tradition, will James forget he's planning a wedding? And will his urban vision for his and Hollie's big day be bold or leave his bride cold?
Bryon Stephens, the president and chief operating officer of Marco's Pizza, goes incognito among his employees to assess how the firm and its franchises are faring.
Greg Adler, the president and CEO of off-road accessory empire 4 Wheel Parts, goes undercover in his own business. But his identity is scrutinised by one canny employee.
Fiesta Sunrise: A cockroach-infested kitchen means that the customers have to leave for their own safety at Fiesta Sunrise, a Mexican restaurant in West Nyack.
Sante La Brea: Gordon visits Sante La Brea, a health food restaurant in Hollywood. But there's nothing glamorous about this eatery as owner Dean faces financial ruin.
...and Selling: Brand new series: Frank and Diana's fixer-upper is far from being fancy. With four kids and a bird, can the twins find them a new nest to call home?
Come On Eileen: Hard-to-please guests challenge the crew. Cat's inexperience frustrates Xandi. And while Barbie gets closer to Kyle, Jared spirals.
Ardal O'Hanlon makes some sheep, Chris Ramsey gets younger, Judi Love becomes a queen, Sophie Duker wets teabags, and Bridget Christie gets lost in a small field.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Sara Pascoe take on Sean Lock and Richard Ayoade. Nick Helm joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Lucy Beaumont take on Roisin Conaty and Joe Wilkinson. Bec Hill joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jeremy's Mummy: Jeremy's mother comes to visit for a family funeral. Her new boyfriend is a military man who Jeremy deeply resents, and who Mark idolises.
Jeremy's Manager: Jeremy and Super Hans get a band manager and play a Christian rock festival. Mark comes along as their roadie and learns how to have sex properly.
Glen covers Rachel's feline failure. Jay tackles Mark's Greek tragedy. Sketch knocks out Emma's punchy cover-up. Alice helps Lois celebrate her penguin persona.
Ardal O'Hanlon makes some sheep, Chris Ramsey gets younger, Judi Love becomes a queen, Sophie Duker wets teabags, and Bridget Christie gets lost in a small field.
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Sara Pascoe take on Sean Lock and Richard Ayoade. Nick Helm joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Teleshopping
Teleshopping
Courtland Gray, chief operating officer of Mississippi-based audio equipment manufacturer Peavey Electronics, goes incognito in his workforce. How do the front-line staff feel?
Todd Pedersen, the founder and CEO of Vivint, a leading solar and home security firm based in Utah, goes undercover among his employees, posing as a reality show contestant.
...Selling: Stuck in real estate limbo, Chandra and Tom have decreased the value of their property. The brothers attempt to raise the bar with an ambitious upgrade. Will it work?
In Gloucestershire, there's a malfunctioning blender, a singing dog, and plenty of catty comments, as Debbie and Paul, Angela and John, and Kirsty and James compete.
In Cheshire, bossy Faye and Chris play it safe with tried-and-tested family favourites, romantic Mark and Shaun serve a wholesome menu, and chatterbox Dawn and Keith plan ambitious dishes.
The Wacky West Country One: What will bride Alex, who loves Pride and Prejudice, make of pig racing and her groom, Mark, in dungarees and wellies on their big day?
The Bungee One: Will bride-to-be Ellie, who hates flying, go along with her groom Craig's heart-stopping ideas?
Ashley and Brandon Wright, the co-owners of Hamburger Mary's, go incognito in their company, to ensure that their culture of diversity is being maintained as the business grows.
Dan Duffy, CEO of the United Real Estate Group, goes undercover in his own firm. He meets different employees and learns about their professional and personal lives.
Cafe 36: Gordon Ramsay visits Cafe 36 in Chicago. Husband-and-wife team Terry and Carol are stuck in the past and their head chef's lax attitude is driving away customers.
Casa Roma: Gordon visits Casa Roma, the oldest restaurant in Lancaster, California. Although the bar is a success, the restaurant is failing and has had 20 chefs in two years.
...and Selling: Brand new series: Nurse Jenna and firefighter Neil must sell his frat house so they can live together. Can his place be revived and smoke out the competition?
Love Me Tender: Jared and Kyle clash. There's a difficult docking. Eileen gets under everyone's skin. And a night in the hot tub has repercussions.
Emotions run high as Bridget Christie shoves a cuddly toy inside a traffic cone. Sophie Duker does a strange dinosaur impression. Plus: robots, and an impressive hose!
Jimmy Carr hosts as Jon Richardson and Joe Wilkinson take on Lee Mack and Katherine Ryan. Brett Domino joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Jimmy Carr hosts, as Sean Lock and Harry Hill take on Jon Richardson and Rosie Jones. Mr Swallow, aka Nick Mohammed, joins Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner. With Rachel Riley.
Mark's Women: Mark gets into online gaming and live role play with Dobby and wonders whether she may really be 'the one'. Meanwhile, Jeremy gets involved with a cult.
Jeremy at JLB: Newly promoted Mark decides to enjoy his power and wealth by splashing out on a boiler and a 'creamy elephant' sofa, and by finding Jeremy a job in the office.
